text:
M5 Section 45(8B) was inserted by section 52 of and paragraph 4 of Schedule 6 to the Family Law Act 1996.


M6 Section 79 was amended by section 157 of and paragraphs 108 and 114(1) and (2) and (3)(b) of Schedule 14 to the Education and Inspections Act 2006 (c.40) .


M7 Section 50 was amended by section 261(1) of and paragraph 131 of Schedule 27 to the Civil Partnership Act 2004.


M8 Section 4(1)(c) was amended by section 111(1) and (2) of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 and section 56 of and paragraphs 21(1) and (2) of Schedule 6 to the Welfare Reform Act 2009 (c.29) .


M9 Section 4ZA was inserted by section 56 of and paragraph 2 of Schedule 6 to the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 2008.


M10 Section 4A(1)(b) was amended by section 75(1) and (2) of the Civil Partnership Act 2004.


M11 Section 14D was inserted by section 115(1) of the Adoption and Children Act 2002.


M12 Section 25 was amended by section 39 of and paragraphs 1 and 15 of Schedule 3 to the Children and Young Persons Act 2008 and section 24 of and paragraph 45 of Schedule 4 to the Access to Justice Act 1999.


M13 Section 14A(1) was inserted by section 115(1) of the Adoption and Children Act 2002.


### CHAPTER 2 E+W GENERAL RULES


### Who the parties are E+W


12.3. —(1) In relation to the proceedings set out in column 1 of the following table, column 2 sets out who may make the application and column 3 sets out who the respondents to those proceedings will be.


(2) The court will direct that a person with parental responsibility be made a party to proceedings where that person requests to be one.


(3) Subject to rule 16.2, the court may at any time direct that—

metadata.title: Part 12 — Children Proceedings except Parental Order Proceedings and Proceedings for Applications in Adoption, Placement and Related Proceedings
